body:
## Summary
- keep comments config in `quartz.ts` on the fixed env contract, with no GitHub/Vercel runner derivation
- add a reusable `resolve-discussion-ids` action that resolves only prepared `GISCUS_REPO` + `GISCUS_CATEGORY`
- prepare comments defaults and PR preview identity in the validate and preview workflows
- update README docs for the fixed contract and local pinned-ID path

## Verification
- `npm run check`
- `npm run build`

Closes #33

comments.body: [vc]: #N0W7yIgwjbbHEpE0od50kJhICS/RVX7qf5ehHByj8LM=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/37nGUfURFueDZrnMgaE4K1rDhUQZ) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-agent-implement-is-a29a53-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-agent-implement-is-a29a53-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 12, 2026 11:20pm |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 94 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to the comments env boundary: workflows, one resolver action, `quartz.ts`, and README. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Keeps Quartz consuming `Comments(...)` and existing env helpers in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/quartz.ts#L90-L203), while workflow prep owns derivation. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Uses explicit `GISCUS_*` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_*` fields rather than deriving from GitHub/Vercel provider vars in Quartz. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Bound transient retries | pass | 7/7 | Resolver retries only transient-looking failures, caps attempts at 3, and warns on deterministic failures in [`action.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids/action.yml#L45-L68). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents the fixed contract, workflow-side defaults, strict/best-effort behavior, and local pinned-ID path. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Product defaults are kept minimal in `quartz.ts`, with repo/category/ID pinning and documented env overrides for common site customization. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep privileged workflows on trusted runtime code | partial | 5/8 | The preview workflow remains a privileged job with `id-token: write` and `deployments: write`, and this PR adds another repo-local action from the checked-out PR branch at [`agent-site-preview.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L61-L62). |

## Notes

- The implementation satisfies issue #33’s core boundary: `quartz.ts` no longer reads the prohibited comments/preview derivation vars, and workflow prep now sets `GISCUS_REPO`, `GISCUS_CATEGORY`, `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`, and `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH`.
- Verified on PR head after `npm ci`: `npm run check` passed and `npm run build` passed. The local build logged `[sepo-comments] disabled: GISCUS_REPO is not set`, matching the documented best-effort default.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Against the trusted-runtime rubric, `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml` still runs repo-local action/runtime code from the PR checkout inside a job with deployment and OIDC privileges. This PR adds `./.github/actions/resolve-discussion-ids` to that same privileged job. Consider isolating unmerged build/runtime code from privileged deploy/status steps in a follow-up.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #34 addresses issue #33 by moving comments and preview environment derivation out of `quartz.ts` and into GitHub workflow preparation.
- The PR adds a reusable `resolve-discussion-ids` action to resolve `GISCUS_REPO` and `GISCUS_CATEGORY` into the IDs required by giscus.
- It also updates preview/validate workflows to prepare comments defaults and preview identity, while documenting the fixed `GISCUS_*`, `SEPO_PREVIEW_*`, and `HYPOTHESIS_*` contract in the README.
- Reviewers agree the core issue #33 boundary is largely satisfied: `quartz.ts` no longer reads the prohibited GitHub/Vercel runner variables.

## Review
- The implementation is close, but one concrete workflow-contract gap should be fixed before merging.
- Other concerns are non-blocking product/default or workflow-hardening judgments that maintainers should acknowledge or track separately.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Workflow export filter includes all `SEPO_*` variables | WARNING | Both workflows forward every `SEPO_*` repo variable even though the fixed build contract is scoped to `SEPO_PREVIEW_*`. This broadens the environment exposed to install/build steps beyond what `quartz.ts` consumes. |
| Comments defaults changed beyond env-derivation scope | WARNING | `quartz.ts` now defaults comments on, enables all tabs, and switches the default trigger to `bot`. The README documents this, but it is broader than issue #33’s narrow env-derivation request and should be intentional. |
| Preview workflow still runs PR-local action code in a privileged job | WARNING | The preview job already has OIDC/deployment permissions, and this PR adds another local action from the PR checkout into that job. The rubric review frames this as a trusted-runtime follow-up risk rather than a merge blocker. |

## Progress
- The current PR conversation and review artifacts were inspected, including the linked issue #33.
- Existing inline review comments and review threads were checked before mutation; none existed.
- Posted one inline comment on `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml:40` covering the shared `SEPO_*` export-filter issue; no replies, minimizations, or thread resolutions were performed.
- Reviewer verification reports `npm run check` and `npm run build` passed on the PR head.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Workflow export filter includes all `SEPO_*` variables</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow filters at [`agent-site-preview.yml#L40`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L40) and [`deploy.yml#L43`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/.github/workflows/deploy.yml#L43) use `^(GISCUS_|SEPO_|HYPOTHESIS_)`, which exports unrelated `SEPO_*` variables.

**Candidate solutions:** Narrow both filters to `^(GISCUS_|SEPO_PREVIEW_|HYPOTHESIS_)`.

**Comments:** This is concrete, localized, and safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Comments defaults changed beyond env-derivation scope</summary>

**Cause:** The PR changes comment behavior defaults in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/quartz.ts#L91): default enabled behavior, all default tabs, and default `bot` trigger mode.

**Candidate solutions:** If intended, explicitly acknowledge the product-default change in the PR/issue context; otherwise restore prior defaults and keep this PR focused on env derivation.

**Comments:** Claude flagged this as a non-blocking policy expansion; the other reviews did not treat it as a code defect.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview workflow still runs PR-local action code in a privileged job</summary>

**Cause:** [`agent-site-preview.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/6ace3d1b2fd382635fa7fb63bf9e1d1d2f0555b8/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L61) runs repo-local action code in a job with preview deployment privileges.

**Candidate solutions:** Track a follow-up to isolate privileged deployment/status work onto trusted runtime code or a separate trusted checkout.

**Comments:** This is broader than the narrow issue #33 fix and was raised by the rubric review as follow-up risk.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
- `FIX_PR`: Narrow the workflow export regex in both workflows; the default-policy and trusted-runtime notes are maintainer/follow-up judgments rather than automation blockers.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Narrow the workflow export filters in `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml` and `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` from `SEPO_*` to `SEPO_PREVIEW_*`.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Update

Updated [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #34.

Added one active rubric:

`rubrics/workflow/keep-consumers-on-fixed-contracts.yaml`

It captures the durable maintainer preference that runtime/application consumers should stick to a fixed documented config contract, while provider-specific parsing, derivation, token use, and lookups belong in workflow or integration prep.

Skipped bot-only review points and one-off implementation details. No commit was created.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

title: Move comments env derivation into workflows
